steps.text: Keep all years whose investments value is greater than 22 billion euros.
Count the qualifying years — 7.
Compare the South Korea and France segments within each Period.
France is ahead in 2014 (Δ −0.26) and only barely in 2015 (Δ −0.04, essentially even), so from 2015 on South Korea is level or ahead — 2015 through Jan-Oct 2019 all count.
Take each year's increase over the previous year's value.
The largest year-over-year increase is at 2010 (35.7 → 50.7, +15.0).
Keep the seasons priced $60 or less (this excludes 2012/13 at $63.1).
Average those six prices: 320.43 ÷ 6 = 53.41.
Filter to the years 2000–2008 and find the second-largest value (0.109 — 2003 and 2004 tie at the top).
Find the smallest value in that range (0.086).
Subtract the smallest from the second-largest: 0.109 − 0.086 = 0.023.
Filter the chart to the years 2010 through 2015 inclusive.
Find Germany's maximum favorable-view percentage in that range: 50, in 2010.
Find the U.S. minimum favorable-view percentage in that range: 19, in 2014.
Subtract the U.S. minimum from the Germany maximum: 50 − 19 = 31.
Find the maximum bar height in the chart (380).
Average all the yearly fire counts (238.75).
Subtract the average from the maximum: 380 − 238.75 = 141.25.
Keep only Hillary Clinton's vote-share bars across all age groups.
Find Scotland's highest SharePercentage across the response-category panels (28).
Find England & Wales's second-lowest SharePercentage (16), treating the 2% outlier as anomalous.
Read off the top three malls' values by US dollars per square foot: 732, 646, and 422.
Add the three values and divide by three: (732 + 646 + 422) ÷ 3 = 600.
Compare Russia's and the US's favorable-view percentages for each year shown.
Keep the years where Russia is at or above the US, including near-ties (within 1 point): 2007, 2009, 2015.
Count those years — 3.
Take the recent years as 2015 through 2020.
Subtract the women's value from the men's value for each year.
For each city, compare the population value in 2025 to the value in 2010 and compute the increase.

Dhaka rises from 14.6 million (2010) to 20.9 million (2025), an increase of 6.3 million, which is larger than the increases for the other cities (e.g., Delhi +6.4? actually 28.6−22.2 = 6.4 is slightly larger; 
Mumbai +5.8, Karachi +5.6). Therefore, Delhi has the biggest jump.
and add them together to get the total sum.
Take the “Very interested” segment values for the non-black races (White, Hispanic, Other).
Average those three values: (0.20 + 0.16 + 0.21) ÷ 3 ≈ 0.19, i.e. 19%.
Keep the years whose FIFA World Ranking position is above 20 and at most 30.
Count the remaining year points to get 10.
Filter to the Europe-region segments (Germany, Great Britain, Spain, Rest of Europe) for the years before 2015.
Sum the Number_of_Employees across those regions and years — 230,769 in total.
First, compute the overall average Consumer Price Index using all the months shown (May ’20 through May ’21).
Then compute the average CPI within each year (average of all 2020 months vs. average of all 2021 months).
Compare how far each year’s average is from the overall average (the absolute deviation).
The 2021 average is farther above the overall average (3.58) than the 2020 average is below it (2.24), so 2021 has the biggest deviation from the total average.
Filter to the Boys group and count the years where the weight exceeds 3670 — 3 years.
Filter to the Girls group and count the years where the weight exceeds 3550 — 5 years.
Add the two counts together: 3 + 5 = 8.

For each year, compare the “Price” value to the “Convenience” value and compute the gap (Price − Convenience). 
The gaps are: 2012 = 20, 2013 = 15, 2014 = 22, 2015 = 16, 2016 = 19.
The smallest gap is 15, which occurs in 2013.
Average the Maximum payment values across all state panels: (25 + 25 + 15 + 15) ÷ 4 = 20.
Average the Minimum payment values across all state panels: (15 + 15 + 10 + 10) ÷ 4 = 12.5.
Subtract the minimum average from the maximum average: 20 − 12.5 = 7.5.
Within each sector's panel, subtract the mid-2013 bar from the 2003 bar to get the decline.
Select the sector with the largest drop: Large companies (9.5 → 5.2, a drop of 4.3).
Focus on the agriculture sector's share across the years.
Its tallest year is 2012 (33.2%).
For each method, compare the “EVERY DAY” share to the “LESS OFTEN” share and take the absolute difference.
Text messaging has 0.55 for every day and 0.13 for less often, giving a difference of 0.42, which is larger than the differences for all other methods.
Find the minimum (1.88 in 2000) and maximum (2.71 in 2020) values.
Average those two values to get 2.295.
Compare that average to the 2010 value (2.39): 2.295 is not greater, so No.
